mcqs.question: What is the primary cause of an accidental fork in a blockchain?

options.text: Simultaneous block mining by different miners

mcqs.explanation: Accidental forks occur when two miners solve a block at nearly the same time, creating temporary competing chains until the network resolves it by adopting the longest chain.

mcqs.question: Which type of fork is backward-compatible with older nodes?
mcqs.explanation: A soft fork is backward-compatible because it tightens the rules, allowing old nodes to still validate blocks created under the new rules.

mcqs.question: What happens to orphan blocks in a blockchain?

options.text: They become part of the main chain
They are discarded and not included in the main chain
They are stored in a separate database

mcqs.explanation: Orphan blocks are valid blocks that are not part of the longest chain and are discarded when the network resolves the fork.

mcqs.question: Which of these is a real-world example of a hard fork?

options.text: Ethereum's transition to Proof of Stake

mcqs.explanation: Both Bitcoin Cash splitting from Bitcoin and Ethereum's transition to Proof of Stake (The Merge) are examples of hard forks as they created permanent divergences in the blockchain.

mcqs.question: What is the main purpose of a soft fork?

options.text: To completely change the consensus mechanism

mcqs.explanation: Soft forks are used to implement protocol upgrades that are backward-compatible, allowing the network to adopt new features without requiring all nodes to upgrade immediately.
